mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample accuracy < 50% and Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of low inflation signals robust supply-side expansion that supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns when applied outside low-inflation regimes, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized signals severe economic deceleration that immediately triggers defensive portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with declining nonfarm payrolls indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling PAYEMS shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX direction, with Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rates during a mid-cycle expansion signal improving labor market tightness that boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns when the signal is applied outside mid-cycle expansion phases, or directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly retail sales growth exceeding 2.0% year-over-year signals robust consumer demand that immediately lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of months where RSAFS YoY growth exceeds 2.0%, or the threshold signal yields Rank IC < 0.02.
